question: The engineer said, 'We will complete the bridge by next December if the weather remains favourable.'
options: The engineer said that they would complete the bridge by the following December if the weather remained favourable.
The engineer said that we would complete the bridge by next December if the weather remains favourable.
The engineer said they will complete the bridge by next December if the weather remained favourable.
The engineer said that they would have completed the bridge by next December if the weather had remained favourable.
explanation: Future tense backshifts to conditional; time expression and conditional clause adjust accordingly.

question: 'The foundation must be strengthened immediately,' the structural expert warned.
options: The structural expert warned that the foundation must be strengthened immediately.
The structural expert warned the foundation to be strengthened immediately.
The structural expert warned that the foundation had to be strengthened immediately.
The structural expert warned to strengthen the foundation immediately.
explanation: Modal 'must' backshifts to 'had to' in reported speech.

question: The professor said, 'Water boils at 100°C at sea level.'
options: The professor said that water boiled at 100°C at sea level.
The professor said that water boils at 100°C at sea level.
The professor said water would boil at 100°C at sea level.
The professor said that water has boiled at 100°C at sea level.
explanation: Universal/scientific truth – no tense backshift.

question: 'Have you submitted the project report yet?' the supervisor asked the intern.
options: The supervisor asked the intern if he had submitted the project report yet.
The supervisor asked the intern if you have submitted the project report.
The supervisor asked the intern whether he submitted the project report.
The supervisor asked the intern had he submitted the project report yet.
explanation: Yes/No question changes to 'if/whether' with past perfect backshift and statement word order.

question: The site engineer said, 'Do not operate the crane until I give clearance.'
options: The site engineer said not to operate the crane until he gave clearance.
The site engineer ordered not to operate the crane until he gives clearance.
The site engineer warned the workers not to operate the crane until he gave clearance.
The site engineer said do not operate the crane until I give clearance.
explanation: Negative imperative with appropriate reporting verb and tense backshift.

question: 'What a magnificent structure this bridge is!' the visiting architect exclaimed.
options: The visiting architect exclaimed what a magnificent structure that bridge was.
The visiting architect exclaimed that it was a magnificent structure.
The visiting architect exclaimed that the bridge is a magnificent structure.
The visiting architect exclaimed what a magnificent structure this bridge is.
explanation: Exclamatory sentence converted using 'that' with necessary pronoun and tense changes.

question: The manager said to us, 'You should verify all calculations before final submission.'
options: The manager advised us that we should verify all calculations before final submission.
The manager told us to verify all calculations before final submission.
The manager said that you should verify all calculations before final submission.
The manager advised us that we should have verified all calculations.
explanation: Modal 'should' remains unchanged; reporting verb 'advised' fits suggestion.

question: 'I had finished the design before the deadline,' claimed the junior engineer.
options: The junior engineer claimed that he had finished the design before the deadline.
The junior engineer claimed that he finished the design before the deadline.

question: The lecturer asked, 'Why did the experiment fail despite following the procedure?'

The lecturer asked why did the experiment fail despite following the procedure.
The lecturer asked why the experiment failed despite following the procedure.
The lecturer asked why the experiment has failed despite following the procedure.

question: 'Let us conduct another trial,' suggested the research team leader.
options: The research team leader suggested that they should conduct another trial.
The research team leader suggested to conduct another trial.
The research team leader suggested let us conduct another trial.

question: The client said, 'I will approve the design only if you reduce the cost by 15 percent.'
options: The client said that he would approve the design only if we reduced the cost by 15 percent.

The client said he would approve the design only if we reduce the cost by 15 percent.
The client said that he would have approved the design if we had reduced the cost.
